message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We need to accept items that are "useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge." Reject ones that are "legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence."

We have to output JSON with verdicts for each candidate_id.

Let's go through each candidate:

1. cand_ffc882deda17882c: Title: "FCU Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control", section 5.22.4. Summary describes control based on zone state with separate gains. Evidence quote from source page 263. This appears to be a valid operational sequence item, grounded in the guideline. Accept.

2. cand_b8f3fd7c8a14acdf: Title: "FCU Fan Speed and SAT Control in Heating Mode", section 5.22.4.3. Summary: When Zone State is Heating, fan speed and SAT setpoint reset based on heating-loop signal. Evidence quote is incomplete: "5.22.4.3.  When the supply fan is proven on,  fan speed and supply air temperature setpoint s are controlled as" — this is cut off and doesn't actually contain the operational details claimed in the summary. The summary seems to be inferred, but the quote doesn't support the specifics. This might be unsupported or misleading. However, the title and section suggest it's part of the sequence. The evidence is truncated; it might be a partial extraction. But the task says: "reject summaries not supported by evidence." Since the evidence doesn't show the heating mode logic, it's not well supported. I'd reject as unsupported. category_if_not: unsupported.

3. cand_0437f120daa2a221: Title: "FCU Fan Speed and SAT Control in Deadband Mode", section 5.22.4.3. Summary: Deadband mode, fan set to DeadbandSpeed, if zero fan off, coils off. Evidence quote again truncated: "5.22.4.3.  When the supply fan is proven on,  fan speed and supply air temperature setpoint s are controlled as" — same issue. Not supported. Reject as unsupported.

4. cand_63a014dbab8c434b: Title: "FCU Fan Speed and SAT Control in Cooling Mode", section 5.22.4.3. Summary: Detailed cooling mode logic. Evidence quote: A detailed quote from page 264 that matches the summary. This is well supported and operational. Accept.

5. cand_056ad5028eb7142a: Title: "FCU Supply Fan Run Condition", section 5.22.4.1. Summary: Fan runs in any mode other than Unoccupied. Evidence quote: exact match, pages 153, 263. Accept.

6. cand_86201fe86c7507f4: Title: "FCU Fan Speed Ramp Limitation", section 5.22.4.2. Summary: 10% per minute ramp. Evidence: exact, pages 153, 263. Accept.

7. cand_640074210a5f5a31: Title: "FCU Maintenance Interval Alarm", section 5.22.5.1. Summary: alarm when fan operated >1500 hours, Level 4, reset on acknowledge. Evidence: matching quote with pages 148, 161, 264. Accept.

8. cand_3e996d7faf8bc453: Title: "FCU Fan Status Alarm", section 5.22.5.2. Summary: status different from command for 15 seconds triggers alarm. Evidence: quote matches, pages 134, 148, 161, 264. Accept.

9. cand_12eaf29b2ab4a7f0: Title: "FCU Filter Pressure Drop Alarm", section 5.22.5.3. Summary: Level 4 alarm when filter DP exceeds larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 min when fan >20% MaxCoolSpeed, DP varies with speed. Evidence quote: "5.22.5.3. Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan" — truncated. However, the summary includes specific formula (DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4) which is not in the quote. The quote only partially supports. The summary might be extrapolating from the table or further details not shown. This could be unsupported. But the title and section suggest it's a valid alarm. However, the evidence is insufficient for the specific formula part. Since the evidence quote is incomplete, it might be a noisy extraction. I'd lean towards rejecting as unsupported because the formula is not in the provided evidence. Category: unsupported.

10. cand_1b37787d0b932976: Title: "FCU AFDD - Too Many Changes in Operating State", section 5.22.6.6. Summary: if changes > max (default 7) in 60 min, fault detected. Evidence: quote "5.22.6.6.  Table 5.20.6.6 shows potential fault conditions that can be evaluated by the AFDD routines. If the" — truncated. The evidence does not contain the specific rule. So not supported. Reject as unsupported.

11. cand_7229c41de76f3e49: Title: "FCU AFDD - SAT Too Low in Full Heating", section 5.22.6.6. Summary: If SAT below setpoint by more than error threshold and heating valve ≥99%, fault detected. Evidence: quote includes the equation and description, with possible diagnoses. Seems well-supported. Accept.

12. cand_3c223cc05629bf00: Similar to above but for cooling. Evidence includes equation and description. Accept.

13. cand_2d1c11a757c742c2: Title: "FCU AFDD - Temperature Drop Across Inactive Cooling Coil", summary: SAT significantly lower than RAT when both valves closed. Evidence: truncated quote. Not supported. Reject as unsupported.

14. cand_7112290d7f11c833: Similar, for heating coil. Truncated evidence. Reject unsupported.

15. cand_5e65221b845cdfc6: Title: "FCU AFDD - Evaluation Suspension on Mode Change", section 5.22.6.9. Summary: Suspended for ModeDelay minutes after mode change. Evidence: "5.22.6.9.  Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:" — truncated, not showing the specific rule. Unsupported. Reject.

16. cand_cc41c8c23f4093b9: Title: "FCU AFDD - Test Mode", section 5.22.6.12. Summary: Test mode sets ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes. Evidence: full quote matching. Accept.

17. cand_7b10442b92f3797f: Title: "FCU AFDD - Alarm Reporting", section 5.22.6.13. Summary: Fault reported as Level 3 with description and possible diagnoses. Evidence: full quote matching. Accept.

18. cand_d1e568e920f7c124: Title: "FCU Chilled-Water Reset Requests Suppression", section 5.22.8.1. Summary: Suppress if fan not at MaxCoolSpeed. Evidence: "All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed." Exact match. Accept.

19. cand_3341754f79767b06: Title: "FCU Chilled-Water Reset Requests", section 5.22.8.1. Summary: logic based on SAT deviation and valve position. Evidence: quote with detailed conditions. Well-supported. Accept.

20. cand_ba4ca32b5c21e1cd: Title: "FCU Chiller Plant Requests", section 5.22.8.2. Summary: send request if CHW valve >95%. Evidence from pages 146,171: quote "a. If the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests." Matches. Accept.

21. cand_6795c1b54cfc68f0: Title: "FCU Hot-Water Reset Requests Suppression", section 5.22.8.3. Summary: suppress if fan not at MaxHeatSpeed. Evidence: "All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed." Accept.

22. cand_a1c9429ff76a70a4: Title: "FCU Hot-Water Reset Requests", section 5.22.8.3. Summary: logic with SAT deviation and valve position. Evidence: detailed quote. Accept.

23. cand_edf7969ffc814b58: Title: "FCU Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests", section 5.22.8.4. Summary: send if HW valve >95%. Evidence: quote matches. Pages: 79,87,91,95,152. Accept.

24. cand_dafa09edc8b94ec6: Title: "FCU AFDD - Operating State Definition", section 5.22.6.2. Summary: OS defined by commanded positions of heating and cooling valves. Evidence: quote matches. Accept.

25. cand_f40653ad2e907284: Title: "FCU AFDD - Points Required", section 5.22.6.3. Summary: SAT, RAT if present, SATsp, HC, CC, FS must be available. Evidence: truncated "5.22.6.3. The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU:" — doesn't list points. Unsupported? But it might be obvious from context; however, evidence doesn't confirm the exact points. Could be unsupported. I'd reject as unsupported.

26. cand_a6a584209f3de30c: Title: "FCU AFDD - Calculated Values", section 5.22.6.4. Summary: 5-min rolling averages of SAT, RAT, ΔOS with 1-min sampling. Evidence: truncated quote. Unsupported. Reject.

27. cand_7f928b90b413b7de: Title: "FCU AFDD - Internal Variables", section 5.22.6.5. Summary: list of variables with default values. Evidence: "The internal variables shown in Table 5.20.6.5 shall be defined... All parameters are adjustable..." but doesn't list them. So the summary is not supported by the provided quote (which refers to a table). Might be unsupported. Reject.

28. cand_d565307387a004a9: Title: "FCU AFDD - Applicable Fault Conditions per OS", section 5.22.6.7. Summary: set depends on OS. Evidence: truncated quote. Unsupported. Reject.

29. cand_23c8bb9db4505041: Title: "FCU AFDD - Operator Suppression of Alarms", section 5.22.6.8. Summary: operator can suppress alarm for any fault condition. Evidence: exact match. Accept.

30. cand_9e0e08de5c7525af: Title: "FCU AFDD - Evaluation Suspension When Not Operating", section 5.22.6.9. Summary: suspended when FCU not operating. Evidence: quote includes "a. When FCU is not operating" but the first part is truncated. However, the quote does show that exact clause. So it's supported. Accept.

31. cand_510fc765e11fcbc7: Title: "FCU AFDD - Non-Applicable Fault Conditions Not Evaluated", section 5.22.6.10. Summary: Fault conditions not applicable to current OS shall not be evaluated. Evidence: exact quote. Accept.

32. cand_cf6151253f77206c: Title: "FCU AFDD - Alarm Delay", section 5.22.6.11. Summary: Fault must be true continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before reported. Evidence: exact quote. Accept.

Now, we need to produce JSON with verdicts. Note: For those we reject, we provide a category_if_not. Allowed values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

We'll label unsupported where evidence is insufficient. For some like cand_f40653ad2e907284, evidence is missing the list, but the summary says what points are needed. The evidence quote is just a partial section header. So "unsupported". Similarly for a6a584209f3de30c, 7f928b90b413b7de, d565307387a004a9. Also cand_12eaf29b2ab4a7f0: evidence truncated, summary includes specific formula not in quote, so unsupported. cand_5e65221b845cdfc6: evidence doesn't show ModeDelay rule, unsupported. cand_2d1c11a757c742c2 and 7112290d7f11c833: truncated, unsupported. cand_1b37787d0b932976: truncated.

For cand_b8f3fd7c8a14acdf and cand_0437f120daa2a221: evidence only shows section header, no details. Reject as unsupported.

All others are accepted.

So final JSON structure: {"verdicts": [{"candidate_id": "...", "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true/false, "reason": "...", "category_if_not": null or appropriate}]}

Let's craft each verdict reason concisely. And output only JSON.
